spans.text: a brief sonnet to beguile my tears
Thy terrors , Spirit of the hoary seas
) Echoed , and startled from his long repose The indignant Phantom of the stormy Cape
that in the winds I hear Thy animating tones
thy gentle state The ancient sceptre of his dread domain
High on the cloudy van of Ararat
the vast circuit of the globe
none Of pasturing herds , or wandering
the lark amid the rainbow's hues

from the North , Where on some snowy promontory's height The Lapland wizard beats his drum , and calls The spirits of the winds , to the utmost South , Where savage Fuego shoots its cold white peaks , Dreariest of lands , and the poor Pecherais Shiver and moan along its waste of snows
to the utmost South , Where savage Fuego shoots its cold white peaks , Dreariest of lands , and the poor Pecherais Shiver and moan along its waste of snows

Fervent and glancing on the friths and sounds
Ten thousand and ten thousand barks
Streaming in white-winged glory to the sun
Their country's dark artillery over the surge Frowning
See from their sad abode , At Heaven's dread voice
The sad survivors of a buried world

a ravine Most mild amid the tempest-riven rocks , Through whose dark pass he saw the flood remote Gray-spreading , while the mists of morn went up

Noah , I come commissioned from above , Where angels move before the eternal throne Of heaven's great King in glory , to dispel The mists of darkness from thy sight ; for know , Not unpermitted of the Eternal One The shadows of thy melancholy dream Hung over thee slumbering : Mine the task to show Futurity's faint scene ; — now follow me
Where angels move before the eternal throne Of heaven's great King in glory , to dispel The mists of darkness from thy sight
The shadows of thy melancholy dream
The shadows of thy melancholy dream

to the unclouded height Of that great Eastern mountain

airy Carmel , rising slow Above the midland main , where hang the capes Of Italy and Greece ; swart Africa , Beneath the parching sun , her long domain
These scenes , OH Noah , thy posterity Shall people
her shapeless idols , vast And smeared with blood , or shrines of lust
maddening as she waves her torch
that love And happiness shall dwell in vales remote
His God , who destined him to noblest ends
Sublime amid the works of God

beam The darkness of the world

divide , Who slumber to the sullen surge , awake , Break forth into thanksgiving , for the bark That rolled upon the desert deep , shall bear The tidings of great joy to all that live , Tidings of life and light

Call rather fiends of hell those who abuse The mercies they receive : that such , indeed , On whom the light of clearer knowledge beams , Should wander forth , and for the tender voice Of charity should scatter crimes and woe , And drench , wherever they pass , the earth with blood , Might make even angels weep : But the poor tribes That groaned and died , deem not them innocent As injured ; more ensanguined rites and deeds Of deepest stain were theirs ; and what if God , So to approve his justice , and exact Most even retribution , blood for blood , Bid forth the Angel of the storm of death
whom the light of clearer knowledge beams , Should wander
the light of clearer knowledge beams ,
more near ! hear the shrill whoop Of murderous war
the shrill whoop Of murderous war
direst hate , And mocking his tormentors , while they gash His flesh unshrinking , tear his eyeballs , burn His beating breast
the dark temples ring To groans and hymns of murderous sacrifice
new sympathies , the forest-chief Shall cast the bleeding hatchet to his gods Of darkness , and one Lord of all
evil for a while To mingle its deep hues and sable shades Amid life's fair perspective
evil for a while To mingle its deep hues and sable shades Amid life's fair perspective
in the end All these shall roll away , and evening
Thou rest in hope , And Him with meekness and with trust
spreading bright his ampler wing , Flew to the heaven of heavens
on the vale Of years departed
the darkness through the labyrinths Of death , where rest her kings
Before whose naval shrine the Memphian host And Pharaoh's pomp were shattered

their midway , yoked to the beaked prows Unseen

With passing tenderness — Live , happy land , Where the poor peasant feels his shed , though small , An independence and a pride , that fill His honest heart with joy — joy such as they Who crowd the mart of men may never feel

The trumpet sounded to the setting sun , That looked from his illumed pavilion , calm Upon the scene of arms , as if , all still , And lovely as his parting light , the world Beneath him spread ; nor clangours , nor deep groans , Were heard , nor victory's shouts , nor sighs , nor shrieks , Were ever wafted from a bleeding land , After the havoc of a conqueror's sword
, nor victory's shouts , nor sighs , nor shrieks , Were ever wafted from a bleeding land

Death is on thy cheek , And thy chill hand the pressure scarce returns Of him , who , agonised and hopeless , hangs With tears and trembling over thee
beneath the tree That fanned its joyous leaves above thy head , Where love had decked the blooming bower , and strewn The sweets of summer
With tears and trembling over thee
